text_source: Ah, good, everyone's here.
Ukon plonks himself down in the middle of the\n
hall, and everyone gathers to sit around him.
What brings you here on such short notice,\n
dear brother?
Well, I think I'm gonna have work for you guys\n
soon. As undercover agents.
Undercover... agents.
No one is quite sure how to react.
Ukon--Oshtor--can't really move in public\n
because of his position as Imperial Guard of\n
the Right...
...So it falls to us to work in his stead.
The only question is, what is he going to make\n
us do...?
Hey, relax a little. It's your first job, so\n
I've picked out a few nice and easy contracts.
Ukon produces a worn, dog-eared notebook,\n
leafing through the pages carefully.
I'll read these off in order, and you guys\n
tell me what sounds good. Starting with...
First up, cleaning up the restroom area in the\n
plaza.
Second one's transporting the, ah, by-products\n
of that process...
Third, stirring the contents of the cesspool\n
so it doesn't stagnate...
And then the fourth job is taking THAT stuff\n
and--
Wait. Wait a second.
What is it?
You can talk around it all you like, but, uh.\n
There seems to be a... common theme to these\n
jobs.
Ah, not much I can do about that.
What... else do you have?
Well, uh. There's one concerning a peruko named\n
Hanako suffering from constipation, wh--
Like I said, why are they all like that?\n
You've gotta have SOMEthing different.
B-Brother...
It may not seem it, but these are the easiest\n
jobs I have for you.
Ukon. Stop fooling around and give us our\n
choices.
I'm not fooling around.
Yeah, you'll have to endure some...\n
unpleasantness. But these are safe, profitable\n
gigs apart from that.
All right, all right, don't give me that\n
murder-stare. Good grief. How about... this one?
Ukon flips to another page, then hands the\n
notebook to Kuon.
A kyorori hunt?
Yep. Job entails going out into the mountains\n
and capturing a wild animal called a kyorori.
Huh. That sounds good, don't you think?
And what exactly is this... kyorori?
They're birds that inhabit the frontier.
Big suckers. Five times a person's height,\n
feathers tough as stone, armor-shredding beak\n
and talons...
Ferociously carnivorous, 'course.
Oh, and there's matarii fishing.
...And how many times larger than a person is\n
this matarii, pray tell?
Oh, not all that, y'know? Like... about yea\n
big.
Ukon holds up his hands, perhaps shoulder-width\n
apart.
Then is it very ferocious, or...?
Nope. Regular old fish.
It's pretty good grilled. Nice and tender.
Um, ah--about matarii, though...?
You know something about them, Kiwru?
It's a... fairly famous delicacy, traded at\n
high prices. U-Usually you serve it at\n
celebrations...
Regular fish that fetch a high price, huh?\n
Sounds like a good deal to me.
Eh? But aren't those...
Ukon watches in silence as we discuss, a smirk\n
on his face.
What's wrong?
Yes, i-if I recall, they only inhabit the\n
frozen seas north of here.
A-And what's more, the waves are routinely\n
rough, and the reefs... Even seasoned sailors\n
often die in those waters.
Nope. Rejecting that one.
Come on, I went through all the trouble of\n
finding deals that work out for you. Don't be\n
a buzzkill.
"Work out for us?" That one is straight-up\n
life-threatening.
Cripes. Picky, picky. Here--there's another\n
contract for capturing a furomun.
Dear brother, isn't that...?
They're extremely venomous. In concentrate,\n
one animal's worth of venom is enough to kill\n
hundreds.
Extreme care is required to handle it, but if\n
prepared properly, its liver can treat heart\n
diseases.
Color me impressed, missy. Its venom can be\n
airborne, too--breathe any of it in, and it\n
can knock you out cold.
...I thought so.
So you don't have anything, like... safer?
Hey, listen. Safe, sweet deals that turn a\n
good profit are hard to come by.
Urgh...
And sanitation is important work.\n
It prevents the spread of disease.
It's a dirty job, but someone's gotta do it.\n
You understand, don't you, kid?
He's got us there.
It's a stroke of luck this is even available!\n
It may be a shitty job, but I trust you'll\n
toil-it through.
...Ukon?
All right, all right. Relax. Let's move onto\n
our main subject for now.
Ukon flashes his typical, fearless smile,\n
posture relaxing slightly.
Main subject?
Yeah. You remember the bandits we captured\n
before?
Bandits... You mean the ones that attacked us\n
on the way to the capital?
Is something up with them?
You could say that. Seems they escaped custody\n
after their court sentencing.
From what I've heard, they still had comrades\n
on the outside who hit their escort during\n
transit.
So... th-those men...
It can only be called carelessness.
A bit of luck, though. Eyewitness reports\n
place them here in the capital, hiding out in\n
the city.
And you want us to find and apprehend them.\n
Am I right?
Is that so...? I see. Is this my first test?
If that's the case, I'll be sure to exceed your\n
expectations!
Well, hold on. I do want you to find them,\n
but when you do, don't try to capture them.\n
Just call me.
Huh?
I don't want you trying to take them on your\n
own.
In other words... Your honor takes a hit if you\n
don't bring them back yourself. Your men, your\n
problem.
Cripes, at least let me get a word in edgewise,\n
here. I shouldn't have to tell you this, but...
They probably remember your faces. If you engage\n
them directly, chances are, they'll want blood.
Truth is, we don't have much more than\n
eyewitness accounts. No hard clues as to where\n
they're hiding.
And they don't want to be found. Searching\n
randomly won't do us any good, but we can't\n
just do nothing.
With all that in mind, I'd like to ask you guys\n
to patrol the capital for me, and to just...\n
stay alert.
If that's all, I don't think we have any reason\n
to turn you down.
Next to the, uh... dirtier requests, this\n
sounds pretty manageable.
And all we have to do if we find anything is\n
contact Ukon. No problem.
All right. We're in.
So, we took the request for the time being,\n
but...
Um... What exactly do we... do now?
Well...
Searching for these bandits at random isn't\n
going to be any kind of efficient.
In this situation, it may be best to put\n
ourselves in their shoes and act based on that.
Thinking of things from their perspective,\n
huh...
To be honest, I can't say I have any clue WHAT\n
they're thinking.
Logically, one would seek out a hiding place\n
to lie low and wait out the storm, but...
If it were me, I'd probably go out and find a\n
way to live it up. Celebrate my newfound\n
freedom.
Of course your mind would go there. Please,\n
try to treat this at least somewhat seriously.
I'm just saying. Knowing me, that's what I'd\n
do.
What manner of imbecile would risk attracting\n
such attention while the law seeks his head?
Eep!
Man
Whup! Sorry, miss. Wasn't payin' attention.
N-No, that's all right... I-I'm sorry...
Don't worry 'bout it. See ya later, cutie.
Henchman
Ah! Hey, boss, come check out this bath!\n
Thing's freakin' HUGE.
Not bad, not bad! Ain't nothin' like a nice\n
drink in a hot bath!
It's been too long since we been on the outside!\n
We gotta live it large. Large, damn it!
Damn straight! Nothing better'n...\n
Ah... Uhm...
Wh--Y-Y-You guys!?
A-Ah...
YOU guys!?
You--You're those--From that one time we--?
N-No... No, no, no, not that damn bird!\n
The nugwisomkami...!
Stay AWAAAAY!!
Th-They're making a break for it!\n
Haku, what do we do!?
Nngh. We have no choice! After them!
Here, of all places--this was supposed to be\n
an easy job!
They're supposed to be in hiding. Why the hell\n
are they nonchalantly walking around like this!?
So. Haku's thought process was identical to\n
theirs, after all.
Get off my case! We have more important things\n
to be worrying about right now.
If they slip through our fingers now, we might\n
never find them again! We can't miss this\n
chance!
